task_completion.description: Open the base rocket design in OpenRocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket application is visible with `clustered_motors.ork` loaded. This can be verified by checking the window title or the design tree.

task_completion.description: Create a new motor configuration simulating the failure of one motor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Motor configurations' tab of OpenRocket, there are at least two configurations. One should be the nominal configuration, and the other should show one less motor igniting (e.g., 3 motors or 6 motors, depending on how the agent handled the base file contradiction).

task_completion.description: Run flight simulations for both nominal and failure configurations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Flight simulations' tab, there are completed simulation runs for both configurations, displaying calculated apogee values and flight data.

task_completion.description: Save the modified design containing both configurations to the target path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket window title shows `clustered_fmea.ork` or a 'Save As' dialog is visible saving the file to `/home/ga/Documents/rockets/clustered_fmea.ork`.

task_completion.description: Write an FMEA incident report comparing apogees and describing flight path consequences.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the file `fmea_report.txt` (saved in `/home/ga/Documents/exports/`). The text must include a comparison of the nominal and failure apogees and a description of the flight path consequences (e.g., asymmetric thrust, off-axis flight).

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the OpenRocket graphical interface (tabs, buttons, simulation runs) rather than just a terminal or text editor modifying the .ork XML file directly.

integrity.description: The apogee values and flight consequences in the report match the actual simulation results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers written in `fmea_report.txt` correspond to the apogee values visible in the OpenRocket 'Flight simulations' tab.

integrity.description: The FMEA report contains original analysis based on the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report text is not just a verbatim copy of the task description, but includes specific numbers and descriptive consequences derived from the simulation.

privileged_info_for_vlm:
Base ORK path: /home/ga/Documents/rockets/clustered_motors.ork
Target ORK path: /home/ga/Documents/rockets/clustered_fmea.ork
Report path: /home/ga/Documents/exports/fmea_report.txt
Note: The task description mentions a 7-motor cluster and a 6-motor failure state, but the actual base file contains 4 motors. The agent may create a failure configuration with either 3 motors (removing 1 from the actual 4) or 6 motors (trying to follow the prompt strictly by adding motors). Both approaches are acceptable as long as a failure configuration with one less motor is created and simulated.
